packages.name: jwt
packages.synopsis: Package jwt is a standard-library-only implementation of JSON Web Tokens (RFC 7519) and the underlying JSON Web Signature compact serialization (RFC 7515).
packages.doc:
Package jwt is a standard-library-only implementation of JSON Web Tokens
(RFC 7519) and the underlying JSON Web Signature compact serialization
(RFC 7515).

# Overview

A JWT is three base64url-encoded, dot-separated parts: a JOSE header, a set
of claims (the payload), and a signature computed over "header.payload". This
package signs and verifies tokens, encodes and validates the registered
claims, and parses PEM key material — all using only the Go standard library
(crypto/*, encoding/*, math/big, ...). There are no third-party dependencies
and no cgo.

# Signing methods

Every algorithm implements the SigningMethod interface. The following are
registered on import:

	HMAC-SHA:    HS256, HS384, HS512   (key: []byte)
	RSA PKCS1v15: RS256, RS384, RS512  (sign: *rsa.PrivateKey, verify: *rsa.PublicKey)
	RSA-PSS:     PS256, PS384, PS512   (sign: *rsa.PrivateKey, verify: *rsa.PublicKey)
	ECDSA:       ES256, ES384, ES512   (sign: *ecdsa.PrivateKey, verify: *ecdsa.PublicKey)
	EdDSA:       EdDSA (Ed25519)       (sign: ed25519.PrivateKey, verify: ed25519.PublicKey)
	Unsecured:   none                  (key: jwt.UnsafeAllowNoneSignatureType)

ECDSA signatures use the fixed-width r||s encoding required by RFC 7518, not
ASN.1 DER. Every method type-asserts its key, so a token cannot be verified
with a key of the wrong type (the classic algorithm-confusion attack). The
"none" method is opt-in twice over: the parser must be given WithAllowNone
and the key must be the sentinel UnsafeAllowNoneSignatureType.

# JWK and JWKS

ParseJWK decodes a single JSON Web Key (RFC 7517) — RSA, EC, OKP (Ed25519),
or oct — into a Go crypto key, and JSONWebKey.Thumbprint computes the RFC 7638
thumbprint. ParseJWKSet decodes a key set; JSONWebKeySet.Keyfunc yields a
Keyfunc that selects a key by the token's "kid". JWKSCache fetches and caches
a remote JWKS over net/http (with an injectable HTTPDoer for tests),
refreshing on TTL expiry or on a kid miss, and also exposes a Keyfunc.

# Detached and unencoded payloads

SignDetached and VerifyDetached implement RFC 7797 ("b64":false): the payload
is signed as-is and carried out of band rather than embedded in the compact
serialization.

# Inspecting without verifying

ParseUnverified decodes a token's header and claims without checking the
signature — useful for reading a "kid" before key selection. Never trust its
output for security decisions.

# Claims

RegisteredClaims models the IANA-registered claims (iss, sub, aud, exp, nbf,
iat, jti). Times use NumericDate (seconds since the Unix epoch) and the
audience uses ClaimStrings, which decodes from either a JSON string or an
array of strings. MapClaims (a map[string]any) handles arbitrary claim sets.
Any custom struct may be used as claims so long as it implements the Claims
interface (a single Valid method); embedding RegisteredClaims is the simplest
way to do so.

# Signing

Build and sign a token in one of two equivalent ways:

	tok := jwt.NewWithClaims(jwt.SigningMethodHS256, claims)
	s, err := tok.SignedString([]byte("secret"))

	s, err := jwt.Sign(claims, jwt.SigningMethodHS256, []byte("secret"))

# Parsing and validation

Parse resolves the verification key through a Keyfunc, which receives the
decoded token (including its header, e.g. "kid") so it can select the correct
key. Parse decodes into MapClaims; ParseWithClaims decodes into a caller-
supplied claims value:

	tok, err := jwt.Parse(s, func(t *jwt.Token) (any, error) {
	        return []byte("secret"), nil
	}, jwt.WithValidMethods([]string{"HS256"}))

Parser options configure validation: WithValidMethods (reject unexpected
algorithms), WithLeeway, WithClock / WithTimeFunc (deterministic time),
WithAudience, WithIssuer, WithSubject, WithIssuedAt, WithExpirationRequired,
WithMaxTokenAge, WithRequiredClaims, WithValidTypes,
WithKnownCriticalHeaders, WithJSONNumber, and WithAllowNone. The exp, nbf,
and (optionally) iat claims are checked against the injected clock with the
configured leeway. Unrecognized JOSE "crit" headers are rejected per
RFC 7515. A Parser built with NewParser may be reused across calls.

All errors are wrapped sentinels; test them with errors.Is, e.g.
errors.Is(err, jwt.ErrTokenExpired) or errors.Is(err, jwt.ErrSignatureInvalid).

# Keys

ParseRSAPrivateKeyFromPEM, ParseRSAPublicKeyFromPEM, ParseECPrivateKeyFromPEM,
ParseECPublicKeyFromPEM, ParseEdPrivateKeyFromPEM, and ParseEdPublicKeyFromPEM
read PEM-encoded key material (PKCS#1, SEC1, PKCS#8, PKIX, and certificates as
applicable).

vars.doc:
Predefined ECDSA signing methods. ES512 uses the P-521 curve, whose field is
521 bits, i.e. 66 bytes per coordinate.

Sentinel errors returned by the package. Callers may test for these using
errors.Is. Parsing and validation errors are typically wrapped so that a
single returned error can match several of these categories.

Predefined RSA-PSS signing methods. Per RFC 7518 the salt length equals the
hash output length.

vars.signature: var ErrKeyMustBePEMEncoded = fmt.Errorf("%w: key must be PEM encoded", ErrInvalidKeyType)
vars.doc: ErrKeyMustBePEMEncoded is returned when the input is not valid PEM.

SigningMethodEdDSA is the shared instance of the EdDSA (Ed25519) signing
method. It is registered under the "EdDSA" alg on import.
SigningMethodNoneAlg is the shared instance of the "none" method.

UnsafeAllowNoneSignatureType is the only key value accepted by the "none"
signing method. Its type makes the opt-in explicit and greppable.

types.name: ClaimStrings
types.signature: type ClaimStrings []string
types.doc:
ClaimStrings represents the JWT "aud" claim, which may be encoded either as a
single string or as an array of strings (RFC 7519 §4.1.3). It always
marshals back as an array unless it holds exactly one element handled by the
caller.

methods.name: MarshalJSON
methods.doc:
MarshalJSON encodes as a single string when there is exactly one audience,
otherwise as an array. An empty value encodes as null (omitted with
omitempty).

methods.name: UnmarshalJSON
methods.doc: UnmarshalJSON accepts either a JSON string or a JSON array of strings.

types.name: Claims
types.doc:
Claims is implemented by any type that can be used as the payload of a JWT.
Valid performs stateless self-validation and is invoked by the parser before
the parser's own option-driven validation runs.

types.name: Clock
types.doc:
Clock supplies the current time to the validator. Injecting a Clock makes
exp/nbf/iat validation deterministic in tests.

types.name: ClockFunc
types.signature: type ClockFunc func() time.Time
types.doc: ClockFunc adapts a function to the Clock interface.

methods.name: Now
methods.doc: Now calls the wrapped function.

types.name: HTTPDoer
types.doc:
HTTPDoer is the subset of *http.Client used by JWKSCache to fetch a key set.
Injecting a custom implementation makes the cache testable without real
network access.

types.name: JSONWebKey
types.doc:
JSONWebKey represents a single JSON Web Key (RFC 7517). It carries the raw
JWK parameters as decoded from JSON and, after ParseJWK (or Parse on a set),
the corresponding Go crypto key in Key.

The supported key types ("kty") are:

	RSA  – *rsa.PublicKey / *rsa.PrivateKey
	EC   – *ecdsa.PublicKey / *ecdsa.PrivateKey (curves P-256, P-384, P-521)
	OKP  – ed25519.PublicKey / ed25519.PrivateKey (curve Ed25519)
	oct  – []byte (a symmetric secret)

funcs.name: NewJSONWebKey
funcs.doc:
NewJSONWebKey constructs a JSONWebKey (RFC 7517) from a Go crypto key,
populating the JSON parameters ("n", "e", "x", "y", "d", "crv", "k", ...) that
correspond to the key's type. It is the inverse of ParseJWK, letting a service
publish its verification keys as a JWK or JWKS.

The supported key types are:

	*rsa.PublicKey / *rsa.PrivateKey        -> kty "RSA"
	*ecdsa.PublicKey / *ecdsa.PrivateKey    -> kty "EC"  (P-256, P-384, P-521)
	ed25519.PublicKey / ed25519.PrivateKey  -> kty "OKP" (crv Ed25519)
	[]byte                                  -> kty "oct"

Coordinate and modulus fields use unpadded base64url with the fixed octet
lengths required by RFC 7518. The resolved key is stored in the Key field, so
the returned JWK round-trips through ParseJWK. Use the chainable WithKeyID,
WithAlgorithm, and WithUse to annotate the key.

funcs.name: ParseJWK
funcs.doc:
ParseJWK decodes a single JWK from its JSON encoding and resolves its Go
crypto key into the returned key's Key field.

methods.name: Base64Thumbprint
methods.doc:
Base64Thumbprint returns the RFC 7638 JWK thumbprint of the key encoded as
unpadded base64url, the textual form conventionally used as a "kid".

methods.name: IsPrivate
methods.doc: IsPrivate reports whether the resolved key is a private (signing) key.

methods.name: MarshalJSON
methods.doc: MarshalJSON encodes the JWK parameters (not the resolved Key) as JSON.

methods.name: Public
methods.doc:
Public returns a JWK holding only the public part of the key. For a symmetric
("oct") key, which has no public form, it returns the receiver unchanged.

methods.name: Thumbprint
methods.doc:
Thumbprint returns the RFC 7638 JWK thumbprint of the key, computed with the
given hash (SHA-256 is the conventional choice). The thumbprint is derived
from the canonical, minimal set of required members for the key type, encoded
as lexicographically ordered compact JSON, and is stable across
implementations. The raw hash digest is returned; base64url-encode it for a
textual identifier.

methods.name: ThumbprintURI
methods.doc:
ThumbprintURI returns the RFC 9278 JWK Thumbprint URI for the key, of the form
"urn:ietf:params:oauth:jwk-thumbprint:sha-256:<base64url>". Only SHA-256,
SHA-384, and SHA-512 have registered hash names; other hashes yield an error.

methods.name: UnmarshalJSON
methods.doc: UnmarshalJSON decodes the JWK JSON parameters and resolves the crypto key.

methods.name: WithAlgorithm
methods.doc:
WithAlgorithm sets the JWK "alg" (intended algorithm, e.g. "RS256") and
returns the receiver for chaining.

methods.name: WithKeyID
methods.doc: WithKeyID sets the JWK "kid" (key ID) and returns the receiver for chaining.

methods.name: WithUse
methods.doc:
WithUse sets the JWK "use" (public key use, "sig" or "enc") and returns the
receiver for chaining.

types.name: JSONWebKeySet
types.doc:
JSONWebKeySet is a set of JSON Web Keys (RFC 7517 §5), the "jwks" document
commonly served at a provider's well-known endpoint.

funcs.name: ParseJWKSet
funcs.doc:
ParseJWKSet decodes a JWKS (JSON Web Key Set) document and resolves the Go
crypto key for every contained JWK.

methods.name: Add
methods.doc:
Add appends keys to the set and returns the receiver for chaining, making it
convenient to assemble a JWKS document for publication.

methods.name: Key
methods.doc:
Key returns the single resolved crypto key in the set matching kid. It fails
with ErrKeyNotFound if no key matches, and with ErrInvalidKey if more than
one key shares the kid (ambiguous). When the set holds exactly one key and
kid is empty, that key is returned.

methods.name: Keyfunc
methods.doc:
Keyfunc returns a Keyfunc that resolves the verification key from the set by
the token's "kid" header. It also enforces that the JWK's declared "alg"
(when present) matches the token's alg header, an additional guard against
algorithm confusion.

methods.name: LookupKeyID
methods.doc:
LookupKeyID returns every key in the set whose Kid equals kid. A JWKS may
legitimately contain more than one key for a kid during key rotation.

types.name: JWKSCache
types.doc:
JWKSCache fetches a JSON Web Key Set from a URL and caches it, refreshing when
the cached copy grows older than the configured TTL or when a lookup misses
(an unknown kid, which typically means the provider rotated keys). It is safe
for concurrent use.

funcs.name: NewJWKSCache
funcs.doc:
NewJWKSCache constructs a cache that fetches the JWKS document at url on
demand. Construction does not perform any network I/O; the first fetch
happens on the first Get, KeySet, or Keyfunc lookup.

methods.name: KeySet
methods.doc:
KeySet returns the cached key set, fetching it if the cache is empty or its
TTL has expired. A refresh failure returns the stale set (if any) alongside
the error so callers may choose to proceed with previously good keys.

methods.name: Keyfunc
methods.doc:
Keyfunc returns a Keyfunc backed by the cache. On a kid miss it triggers one
rate-limited refresh (bounded by WithMinRefreshInterval) before giving up, so
key rotation is picked up without a fetch storm. The context used for any
refresh is context.Background; use KeyfuncCtx to supply your own.

methods.name: KeyfuncCtx
methods.doc: KeyfuncCtx is like Keyfunc but uses ctx for any refresh network calls.

methods.name: Refresh
methods.doc:
Refresh forces an immediate re-fetch of the JWKS, ignoring the TTL. It is
useful to warm the cache at startup.

types.name: JWKSCacheOption
types.signature: type JWKSCacheOption func(*JWKSCache)
types.doc: JWKSCacheOption configures a JWKSCache.

funcs.name: WithHTTPClient
funcs.doc:
WithHTTPClient sets the HTTP client used to fetch the JWKS. The default is
http.DefaultClient. Pass any HTTPDoer (e.g. a fake) to control fetching in
tests.

funcs.name: WithMinRefreshInterval
funcs.doc:
WithMinRefreshInterval rate-limits refreshes triggered by an unknown kid, so
a burst of tokens with a bogus kid cannot cause a fetch storm. The default is
one minute.

funcs.name: WithRefreshInterval
funcs.doc:
WithRefreshInterval sets how long a fetched key set is considered fresh.
After this TTL elapses the next lookup triggers a refresh. The default is one
hour.

types.name: Keyfunc
types.signature: type Keyfunc func(token *Token) (any, error)
types.doc:
Keyfunc is called by the parser after the header is decoded, with the
partially constructed token, to obtain the verification key. Implementations
typically inspect token.Header (e.g. the "kid") and/or token.Method to
choose the key. Returning an error aborts parsing.

types.name: MapClaims
types.signature: type MapClaims map[string]any
types.doc:
MapClaims is a Claims implementation backed by a plain map, useful when the
set of claims is dynamic or not known ahead of time.

methods.name: GetAudience
methods.doc: GetAudience returns the aud claim, tolerating both string and []any forms.

methods.name: GetAuthorizedParty
methods.doc:
GetAuthorizedParty returns the OpenID Connect "azp" (authorized party) claim
as a string, or "" if absent. When present it names the party the token was
issued to, and should equal the relying party's client ID.

methods.name: GetBool
methods.doc:
GetBool returns the claim key as a bool together with true when it is present
and holds a JSON boolean. It returns (false, false) otherwise.

methods.name: GetExpirationTime
methods.doc: GetExpirationTime returns the exp claim.

methods.name: GetFloat64
methods.doc:
GetFloat64 returns the claim key as a float64 together with true when it is
present and numeric (float64, json.Number, or an integer type). It returns
(0, false) otherwise.

methods.name: GetID
methods.doc: GetID returns the jti (JWT ID) claim as a string, or "" if absent.

methods.name: GetInt64
methods.doc:
GetInt64 returns the claim key as an int64 together with true when it is
present and holds an integral numeric value (float64, json.Number, or an
integer type). It returns (0, false) otherwise.

methods.name: GetIssuedAt
methods.doc: GetIssuedAt returns the iat claim.

methods.name: GetIssuer
methods.doc: GetIssuer returns the iss claim as a string, or "" if absent or non-string.

methods.name: GetNonce
methods.doc:
GetNonce returns the OpenID Connect "nonce" claim as a string, or "" if
absent. The nonce binds a token to a client session and must be compared by
the caller against the value it sent.

methods.name: GetNotBefore
methods.doc: GetNotBefore returns the nbf claim.

methods.name: GetString
methods.doc: GetString returns claim key as a string, or "" if absent or not a string.

methods.name: GetStringSlice
methods.doc:
GetStringSlice returns the claim key as a slice of strings. A single JSON
string yields a one-element slice; a JSON array yields its string elements
(non-string elements are skipped). A missing or unsupported value yields nil.

methods.name: GetSubject
methods.doc: GetSubject returns the sub claim as a string, or "" if absent or non-string.

methods.name: GetTime
methods.doc:
GetTime returns the claim key interpreted as a NumericDate (seconds since the
Unix epoch) together with true when it is present and numeric. It returns the
zero time and false otherwise. The returned time is in UTC.

methods.name: Has
methods.doc: Has reports whether the claim key is present with a non-nil value.

methods.name: Set
methods.signature: func (m MapClaims) Set(key string, value any) MapClaims
methods.doc:
Set stores value under key and returns the receiver, allowing calls to be
chained when assembling a claim set (m.Set("a", 1).Set("b", 2)).

methods.name: Valid
methods.doc:
Valid satisfies the Claims interface. As with RegisteredClaims, time checks
are delegated to the parser's validator, so this returns nil.

methods.name: VerifyAudience
methods.signature: func (m MapClaims) VerifyAudience(cmp string, req bool) bool
methods.doc:
VerifyAudience reports whether the "aud" claim contains cmp. When req is false
and the claim is absent, verification passes; when req is true, an absent
claim fails. This mirrors the golang-jwt v4 MapClaims API.

methods.name: VerifyExpiresAt
methods.signature: func (m MapClaims) VerifyExpiresAt(cmp int64, req bool) bool
methods.doc:
VerifyExpiresAt reports whether the token is unexpired relative to cmp (a Unix
timestamp in seconds): it passes when exp is strictly after cmp. When req is
false and the claim is absent, verification passes; when req is true, an
absent claim fails.

methods.name: VerifyIssuedAt
methods.signature: func (m MapClaims) VerifyIssuedAt(cmp int64, req bool) bool
methods.doc:
VerifyIssuedAt reports whether the "iat" claim is at or before cmp (a Unix
timestamp in seconds), i.e. the token was not issued in the future relative to
cmp. When req is false and the claim is absent, verification passes; when req
is true, an absent claim fails.

methods.name: VerifyIssuer
methods.signature: func (m MapClaims) VerifyIssuer(cmp string, req bool) bool
methods.doc:
VerifyIssuer reports whether the "iss" claim equals cmp. When req is false and
the claim is absent, verification passes; when req is true, an absent claim
fails.

methods.name: VerifyNotBefore
methods.signature: func (m MapClaims) VerifyNotBefore(cmp int64, req bool) bool
methods.doc:
VerifyNotBefore reports whether the "nbf" claim is at or before cmp (a Unix
timestamp in seconds), i.e. the token is already valid relative to cmp. When
req is false and the claim is absent, verification passes; when req is true,
an absent claim fails.

types.name: NumericDate
types.doc:
NumericDate wraps time.Time to marshal as a JSON numeric date: the number of
seconds since the Unix epoch, per RFC 7519 §2. Fractional seconds are
truncated on encode.

funcs.name: NewNumericDate
funcs.doc: NewNumericDate returns a *NumericDate for t, or nil if t is the zero time.

methods.name: MarshalJSON
methods.doc: MarshalJSON encodes the date as an integer number of seconds since the epoch.

methods.name: UnmarshalJSON
methods.doc: UnmarshalJSON decodes a JSON number (possibly fractional) into the date.

types.name: Parser
types.doc:
Parser parses and validates tokens according to its configured options. The
zero value is usable but NewParser with options is the common path.

funcs.name: NewParser
funcs.doc: NewParser constructs a Parser with the given options.

methods.name: Parse
methods.signature: func (p *Parser) Parse(tokenString string, keyFunc Keyfunc) (*Token, error)
methods.doc: Parse is the method form; it decodes into MapClaims.

methods.name: ParseUnverified
methods.signature: func (p *Parser) ParseUnverified(tokenString string, claims Claims) (*Token, []string, error)
methods.doc:
ParseUnverified decodes a token's header and claims WITHOUT verifying the
signature or validating the claims. It is useful for inspecting a token
(e.g. reading the "kid" or "iss" to choose a key or key set) before
verification. The returned token has Valid == false and its Signature
decoded but unchecked.

SECURITY: never trust the claims of a token returned by ParseUnverified. Use
Parse or ParseWithClaims for any token whose contents drive a decision.

methods.name: ParseWithClaims
methods.signature: func (p *Parser) ParseWithClaims(tokenString string, claims Claims, keyFunc Keyfunc) (*Token, error)
methods.doc:
ParseWithClaims decodes the token, resolves the key, verifies the signature,
and validates the claims. On any failure it returns a nil-safe token (the
partially parsed token may be returned alongside the error for inspection)
and a wrapped error.

types.name: ParserOption
types.signature: type ParserOption func(*Parser)
types.doc: ParserOption configures a Parser.

funcs.name: WithAllowNone
funcs.doc:
WithAllowNone permits the unsecured "none" algorithm. Without this option a
token whose alg is "none" is rejected outright. Even with it enabled, the
keyFunc must return UnsafeAllowNoneSignatureType as the key.

funcs.name: WithAudience
funcs.doc: WithAudience requires that the aud claim contain aud.

funcs.name: WithClock
funcs.doc:
WithClock injects the clock used for time-based validation, enabling
deterministic tests.

funcs.name: WithExpirationRequired
funcs.doc: WithExpirationRequired rejects tokens that lack an exp claim.

funcs.name: WithIgnoreExpiration
funcs.doc:
WithIgnoreExpiration disables the exp (expiration) claim check while leaving
every other validation — signature, nbf, iat, audience, issuer, subject and
required-claim checks — in force. It mirrors the "ignoreExpiration" option of
node's jsonwebtoken and is narrower than WithoutClaimsValidation, which
suppresses all claim checks. Because WithExpirationRequired is itself part of
the exp check, it too is suppressed when expiration is ignored.

funcs.name: WithIgnoreNotBefore
funcs.doc:
WithIgnoreNotBefore disables the nbf (not-before) claim check while leaving
every other validation in force. It mirrors the "ignoreNotBefore" option of
node's jsonwebtoken, letting a caller accept a token whose validity window
has not yet opened without also relaxing the exp, audience, issuer or subject
checks.

funcs.name: WithIssuedAt
funcs.doc: WithIssuedAt enables validation that iat is not in the future.

funcs.name: WithIssuer
funcs.doc: WithIssuer requires that the iss claim equal iss.

funcs.name: WithJSONNumber
funcs.doc:
WithJSONNumber decodes claim numbers into json.Number rather than float64,
preserving integer precision for large values. Applies to MapClaims.

funcs.name: WithKnownCriticalHeaders
funcs.doc:
WithKnownCriticalHeaders registers additional JOSE "crit" header extension
names the caller understands and will process out of band. By default only
"b64" (RFC 7797) is recognized; any unrecognized critical header causes the
token to be rejected, as required by RFC 7515 §4.1.11.

funcs.name: WithLeeway
funcs.doc: WithLeeway sets a symmetric tolerance applied to exp, nbf, and iat checks.

funcs.name: WithMaxTokenAge
funcs.doc:
WithMaxTokenAge rejects a token whose age — the difference between the
verification time and its iat claim — exceeds d. The iat claim becomes
required; a token without it is rejected. This mirrors the "maxAge" option of
node's jsonwebtoken.

funcs.name: WithPaddingAllowed
funcs.doc:
WithPaddingAllowed configures the parser to accept base64url segments that
carry '=' padding, tolerating producers that violate the unpadded-base64url
requirement of the compact serialization. This is the parser's default
behavior; the option exists for API symmetry and to re-enable tolerance on a
parser that a caller (or a reused base Parser) previously put into strict
mode with WithStrictDecoding.

funcs.name: WithRequiredClaims
funcs.doc:
WithRequiredClaims rejects a token that does not carry every named claim (with
a non-null value). Use it to insist on, e.g., "jti" or a custom claim
regardless of the claims type in use.

funcs.name: WithStrictDecoding
funcs.doc:
WithStrictDecoding configures the parser to reject any base64url segment that
carries '=' padding characters. The JWS compact serialization mandates
unpadded base64url (RFC 7515 §2, RFC 7797), so strict decoding matches the
specification exactly. It is the opposite of WithPaddingAllowed; the last of
the two options applied wins.

funcs.name: WithSubject
funcs.doc: WithSubject requires that the sub claim equal sub.

funcs.name: WithTimeFunc
funcs.doc: WithTimeFunc injects a time source as a function.

funcs.name: WithValidMethods
funcs.doc:
WithValidMethods restricts the accepted alg header values to algs. This is
the primary defense against algorithm-confusion attacks and should be set in
production.

funcs.name: WithValidTypes
funcs.doc:
WithValidTypes restricts the accepted JOSE "typ" header values to types. A
token whose typ header is present but not listed is rejected; a token with no
typ header is accepted (the header is optional per RFC 7519). Comparison is
case-insensitive, matching how "JWT" is conventionally written.

funcs.name: WithoutClaimsValidation
funcs.doc:
WithoutClaimsValidation disables all claim validation performed after the
signature is verified: the Claims.Valid self-check, the registered time and
equality checks (exp, nbf, iat, aud, iss, sub, max-token-age), and the
WithRequiredClaims presence checks are all skipped. The signature is still
verified, so the token's authenticity is unaffected; only the semantic
validity of its claims is left to the caller. Use this when the application
enforces its own claim policy, or to inspect the claims of an authentic but
possibly expired token.

types.name: RegisteredClaims
types.signature:
type RegisteredClaims struct {
	Issuer    string       `json:"iss,omitempty"`
	Subject   string       `json:"sub,omitempty"`
	Audience  ClaimStrings `json:"aud,omitempty"`
	ExpiresAt *NumericDate `json:"exp,omitempty"`
	NotBefore *NumericDate `json:"nbf,omitempty"`
	IssuedAt  *NumericDate `json:"iat,omitempty"`
	ID        string       `json:"jti,omitempty"`
}
types.doc:
RegisteredClaims holds the IANA-registered claim names from RFC 7519 §4.1.
All fields are optional and omitted from the encoding when empty.

methods.name: GetAudience
methods.doc: GetAudience returns the aud claim.

methods.name: GetExpirationTime
methods.doc: GetExpirationTime returns the exp claim.

methods.name: GetID
methods.doc:
GetID returns the "jti" (JWT ID) claim, giving RegisteredClaims the same
accessor MapClaims already exposes. It completes the registered-claim getter
set for callers that treat claims uniformly.

methods.name: GetIssuedAt
methods.doc: GetIssuedAt returns the iat claim.

methods.name: GetIssuer
methods.doc: GetIssuer returns the iss claim.

methods.name: GetNotBefore
methods.doc: GetNotBefore returns the nbf claim.

methods.name: GetSubject
methods.doc: GetSubject returns the sub claim.

methods.name: Valid
methods.doc:
Valid satisfies the Claims interface. Time-based validation is performed by
the parser's validator (with configurable leeway and clock), so this returns
nil and defers to that machinery.

types.name: SigningMethod
types.doc:
SigningMethod defines the interface implemented by every algorithm the
library supports. Alg returns the value written to the JOSE "alg" header,
Sign produces a signature over the signing input, and Verify checks a
signature against that input.

funcs.name: GetSigningMethod
funcs.doc:
GetSigningMethod returns the SigningMethod registered for alg, or nil if none
is registered.

types.name: SigningMethodECDSA
types.doc:
SigningMethodECDSA implements ECDSA (ES256, ES384, ES512). Signatures use the
fixed-width r||s concatenation format defined by RFC 7518, not the ASN.1 DER
form produced by ecdsa.SignASN1. Signing keys are *ecdsa.PrivateKey and
verification keys are *ecdsa.PublicKey.

methods.name: Alg
methods.doc: Alg returns the JWS alg identifier.

methods.name: Sign
methods.signature: func (m *SigningMethodECDSA) Sign(signingString string, key any) ([]byte, error)
methods.doc:
Sign signs signingString with an *ecdsa.PrivateKey and returns the r||s
fixed-width encoding.

methods.name: Verify
methods.signature: func (m *SigningMethodECDSA) Verify(signingString string, sig []byte, key any) error
methods.doc: Verify verifies an r||s fixed-width ECDSA signature with an *ecdsa.PublicKey.

types.name: SigningMethodEd25519
types.signature: type SigningMethodEd25519 struct{}
types.doc:
SigningMethodEd25519 implements the EdDSA signing method using the Ed25519
curve (RFC 8037). The JWS "alg" header value is "EdDSA". Signing keys are
ed25519.PrivateKey and verification keys are ed25519.PublicKey.

methods.name: Alg
methods.doc: Alg returns "EdDSA", the JWS alg identifier for Ed25519 signatures.

methods.name: Sign
methods.signature: func (m *SigningMethodEd25519) Sign(signingString string, key any) ([]byte, error)
methods.doc:
Sign signs signingString with an ed25519.PrivateKey and returns the raw
64-byte signature. The key must be an ed25519.PrivateKey; any other type
(including a pointer or an RSA/EC key) is rejected with ErrInvalidKeyType,
which closes the algorithm-confusion attack surface for this method.

methods.name: Verify
methods.signature: func (m *SigningMethodEd25519) Verify(signingString string, sig []byte, key any) error
methods.doc:
Verify checks an Ed25519 signature over signingString with an
ed25519.PublicKey. The key must be an ed25519.PublicKey; any other type is
rejected with ErrInvalidKeyType.

types.name: SigningMethodHMAC
types.doc:
SigningMethodHMAC implements the HMAC-SHA family (HS256, HS384, HS512).
The signing key must be a []byte secret.

methods.name: Alg
methods.doc: Alg returns the JWS alg identifier.

methods.name: Sign
methods.signature: func (m *SigningMethodHMAC) Sign(signingString string, key any) ([]byte, error)
methods.doc: Sign computes HMAC(key, signingString). key must be a []byte.

methods.name: Verify
methods.signature: func (m *SigningMethodHMAC) Verify(signingString string, sig []byte, key any) error
methods.doc: Verify recomputes the MAC and compares it in constant time.

types.name: SigningMethodNone
types.signature: type SigningMethodNone struct{}
types.doc:
SigningMethodNone implements the unsecured "none" algorithm from RFC 7515.
It produces an empty signature. To guard against the classic algorithm-
confusion attack, both Sign and Verify require the caller to pass
UnsafeAllowNoneSignatureType as the key.

methods.name: Alg
methods.doc: Alg returns "none".

methods.name: Sign
methods.signature: func (m *SigningMethodNone) Sign(_ string, key any) ([]byte, error)
methods.doc: Sign returns an empty signature. key must be UnsafeAllowNoneSignatureType.

methods.name: Verify
methods.signature: func (m *SigningMethodNone) Verify(_ string, sig []byte, key any) error
methods.doc:
Verify accepts only an empty signature and only when key is
UnsafeAllowNoneSignatureType.

types.name: SigningMethodRSA
types.doc:
SigningMethodRSA implements RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5 (RS256, RS384, RS512).
Signing keys are *rsa.PrivateKey and verification keys are *rsa.PublicKey.

methods.name: Alg
methods.doc: Alg returns the JWS alg identifier.

methods.name: Sign
methods.signature: func (m *SigningMethodRSA) Sign(signingString string, key any) ([]byte, error)
methods.doc: Sign signs signingString with an *rsa.PrivateKey using PKCS1v15.

methods.name: Verify
methods.signature: func (m *SigningMethodRSA) Verify(signingString string, sig []byte, key any) error
methods.doc: Verify verifies a PKCS1v15 signature with an *rsa.PublicKey.

types.name: SigningMethodRSAPSS
types.doc:
SigningMethodRSAPSS implements RSASSA-PSS (PS256, PS384, PS512).
Signing keys are *rsa.PrivateKey and verification keys are *rsa.PublicKey.

methods.name: Alg
methods.doc: Alg returns the JWS alg identifier.

methods.name: Sign
methods.signature: func (m *SigningMethodRSAPSS) Sign(signingString string, key any) ([]byte, error)
methods.doc: Sign signs signingString with an *rsa.PrivateKey using RSA-PSS.

methods.name: Verify
methods.signature: func (m *SigningMethodRSAPSS) Verify(signingString string, sig []byte, key any) error
methods.doc: Verify verifies an RSA-PSS signature with an *rsa.PublicKey.

types.name: Token
types.doc:
Token represents a JWT. Before signing it holds the header and claims to be
serialized; after parsing it additionally holds the raw compact form and the
decoded signature.

funcs.name: New
funcs.doc:
New returns a Token that will be signed with method, using RegisteredClaims
as an empty default payload. Use NewWithClaims to supply claims.

funcs.name: NewWithClaims
funcs.doc:
NewWithClaims returns a Token with the given method and claims and a standard
JOSE header ({"alg":..., "typ":"JWT"}).

funcs.name: Parse
funcs.signature: func Parse(tokenString string, keyFunc Keyfunc, opts ...ParserOption) (*Token, error)
funcs.doc:
Parse parses a compact JWT into a Token with MapClaims, using keyFunc to
resolve the verification key, then verifies the signature and validates the
claims. See ParseWithClaims to decode into a specific claims type.

funcs.name: ParseUnverified
funcs.signature: func ParseUnverified(tokenString string, claims Claims) (*Token, []string, error)
funcs.doc:
ParseUnverified is the package-level convenience form of
Parser.ParseUnverified, decoding into the supplied claims value without
verifying the signature. See the method for the security caveat.

funcs.name: ParseWithClaims
funcs.signature: func ParseWithClaims(tokenString string, claims Claims, keyFunc Keyfunc, opts ...ParserOption) (*Token, error)
funcs.doc:
ParseWithClaims parses into the supplied claims value (which is populated in
place), verifies the signature via keyFunc, and validates the claims.

funcs.name: VerifyDetached
funcs.signature: func VerifyDetached(jws string, payload []byte, keyFunc Keyfunc, opts ...ParserOption) (*Token, error)
funcs.doc:
VerifyDetached verifies a detached JWS produced with an unencoded payload
(RFC 7797). jws is the "BASE64URL(header)..BASE64URL(signature)"
serialization and payload is the detached payload supplied out of band.
keyFunc resolves the verification key from the decoded header.

Parser options that concern algorithm selection are honored: WithValidMethods
restricts the accepted alg (the primary algorithm-confusion defense) and
WithAllowNone is required to accept alg "none". Claim validation options do
not apply, since the payload is opaque bytes rather than JSON claims.

methods.name: HeaderString
methods.doc:
HeaderString returns the header parameter key as a string, or "" if it is
absent or not a string.

methods.name: KeyID
methods.doc:
KeyID returns the token's "kid" header as a string, or "" if it is absent or
not a string. It is the read counterpart to SetKID and is convenient after
ParseUnverified to select a verification key.

methods.name: SetHeader
methods.doc:
SetHeader sets an arbitrary JOSE header parameter and returns the token for
chaining. It is the general form of SetKID; use it to add headers such as
"cty", "x5t", or a custom extension before signing. The header map is
allocated if the token does not yet have one.

methods.name: SetKID
methods.doc: SetKID sets the "kid" (key ID) JOSE header, returning the token for chaining.

methods.name: SetType
methods.doc:
SetType sets the JOSE "typ" header (for example "JWT" or "at+jwt") and returns
the token for chaining.

methods.name: SignedString
methods.doc:
SignedString signs the token with key and returns the complete compact
serialization (header.payload.signature).

methods.name: SigningString
methods.doc:
SigningString returns the signing input: base64url(header) + "." +
base64url(payload). This is the value passed to SigningMethod.Sign/Verify.

methods.name: String
methods.doc:
String returns the compact serialization of the token. For a token produced
by the parser it returns the original Raw string, giving a lossless
parse/serialize round-trip. For a token built with New/NewWithClaims that has
not yet been signed, Raw is empty and String returns "".

methods.name: TokenType
methods.doc:
TokenType returns the token's "typ" header as a string, or "" if it is absent
or not a string.

types.name: Validator
types.doc:
Validator validates a set of claims independently of parsing and signature
verification. It is configured with the same time- and equality-based
ParserOptions used by Parse (WithLeeway, WithClock, WithTimeFunc,
WithAudience, WithIssuer, WithSubject, WithIssuedAt, WithExpirationRequired,
WithMaxTokenAge, WithRequiredClaims), letting a caller re-run the parser's
claim checks against claims obtained elsewhere — for example after
ParseUnverified, or against claims assembled by the application. A Validator
is safe for concurrent use as long as its injected Clock is.

funcs.name: NewValidator
funcs.doc:
NewValidator builds a Validator from the given options. Only the validation
options listed on the Validator type have any effect; options that concern
signing methods, decoding, or header handling are accepted but ignored.

methods.name: Validate
methods.doc:
Validate runs the configured checks against claims and returns nil if they
all pass. It first invokes claims.Valid (the type's own self-check), then, if
the claims expose the registered getters (as RegisteredClaims and MapClaims
do), the time and equality checks, and finally the required-claim presence
checks. Multiple failures are joined so the result can be tested for any of
them with errors.Is.

funcs.name: ComputeKeyID
funcs.doc:
ComputeKeyID derives a stable key identifier from a Go crypto key: the
base64url-encoded SHA-256 RFC 7638 thumbprint of the key's public part. Using
it as the "kid" gives every key a deterministic, collision-resistant name
that both signer and verifier can compute independently.

funcs.name: DecodeSegment
funcs.doc:
DecodeSegment decodes a base64url segment without padding. For robustness it
also tolerates segments that carry '=' padding by falling back to the padded
decoder, since some producers emit padding in violation of the spec.

funcs.name: EncodeECPrivateKeyToPEM
funcs.doc:
EncodeECPrivateKeyToPEM serializes an ECDSA private key to PEM using the SEC1
("EC PRIVATE KEY") encoding, the inverse of ParseECPrivateKeyFromPEM.

funcs.name: EncodeECPublicKeyToPEM
funcs.doc:
EncodeECPublicKeyToPEM serializes an ECDSA public key to PEM using the PKIX
("PUBLIC KEY") encoding, the inverse of ParseECPublicKeyFromPEM.

funcs.name: EncodeEdPrivateKeyToPEM
funcs.doc:
EncodeEdPrivateKeyToPEM serializes an Ed25519 private key to PEM using the
PKCS#8 ("PRIVATE KEY") encoding, the inverse of ParseEdPrivateKeyFromPEM.

funcs.name: EncodeEdPublicKeyToPEM
funcs.doc:
EncodeEdPublicKeyToPEM serializes an Ed25519 public key to PEM using the PKIX
("PUBLIC KEY") encoding, the inverse of ParseEdPublicKeyFromPEM.

funcs.name: EncodePrivateKeyToPEM
funcs.doc:
EncodePrivateKeyToPEM serializes any supported private key (*rsa.PrivateKey,
*ecdsa.PrivateKey, or ed25519.PrivateKey) to a PKCS#8 ("PRIVATE KEY") PEM
block. Unlike the type-specific encoders, it always uses PKCS#8, giving a
single uniform private-key file format across algorithms.

funcs.name: EncodePublicKeyToPEM
funcs.doc:
EncodePublicKeyToPEM serializes any supported public key (*rsa.PublicKey,
*ecdsa.PublicKey, or ed25519.PublicKey) to a PKIX ("PUBLIC KEY") PEM block. It
is the general-purpose counterpart to the type-specific encoders above.

funcs.name: EncodeRSAPrivateKeyToPEM
funcs.doc:
EncodeRSAPrivateKeyToPEM serializes an RSA private key to PEM using the PKCS#1
("RSA PRIVATE KEY") encoding, the inverse of ParseRSAPrivateKeyFromPEM. It
never fails for a valid key.

funcs.name: EncodeRSAPublicKeyToPEM
funcs.doc:
EncodeRSAPublicKeyToPEM serializes an RSA public key to PEM using the PKIX
("PUBLIC KEY") encoding, the inverse of ParseRSAPublicKeyFromPEM.

funcs.name: EncodeSegment
funcs.doc:
EncodeSegment returns the base64url (RFC 4648 §5) encoding of seg without
padding, as required by JWS.

funcs.name: GetAlgorithms
funcs.doc:
GetAlgorithms returns the sorted-order-independent list of registered alg
identifiers. It is primarily useful for diagnostics and tests.

funcs.name: ParseECPrivateKeyFromPEM
funcs.doc:
ParseECPrivateKeyFromPEM parses a PEM-encoded EC private key in either SEC1
("EC PRIVATE KEY") or PKCS#8 ("PRIVATE KEY") form.

funcs.name: ParseECPublicKeyFromPEM
funcs.doc:
ParseECPublicKeyFromPEM parses a PEM-encoded EC public key (PKIX
"PUBLIC KEY") or a certificate whose public key is EC.

funcs.name: ParseEdPrivateKeyFromPEM
funcs.doc:
ParseEdPrivateKeyFromPEM parses a PEM-encoded Ed25519 private key in PKCS#8
("PRIVATE KEY") form and returns it as an ed25519.PrivateKey.

funcs.name: ParseEdPublicKeyFromPEM
funcs.doc:
ParseEdPublicKeyFromPEM parses a PEM-encoded Ed25519 public key (PKIX
"PUBLIC KEY") or a certificate whose public key is Ed25519, returning it as
an ed25519.PublicKey.

funcs.name: ParseRSAPrivateKeyFromPEM
funcs.doc:
ParseRSAPrivateKeyFromPEM parses a PEM-encoded RSA private key in either
PKCS#1 ("RSA PRIVATE KEY") or PKCS#8 ("PRIVATE KEY") form.

funcs.name: ParseRSAPublicKeyFromPEM
funcs.doc:
ParseRSAPublicKeyFromPEM parses a PEM-encoded RSA public key, accepting both
PKIX ("PUBLIC KEY") and PKCS#1 ("RSA PUBLIC KEY") forms, and also a
certificate ("CERTIFICATE") whose public key is RSA.

funcs.name: RegisterSigningMethod
funcs.signature: func RegisterSigningMethod(alg string, f func() SigningMethod)
funcs.doc:
RegisterSigningMethod registers alg so it can be resolved by GetSigningMethod
and by the parser. It is safe for concurrent use. Registering the same alg
twice replaces the prior registration.

funcs.name: Sign
funcs.signature: func Sign(claims Claims, method SigningMethod, key any) (string, error)
funcs.doc:
Sign is a convenience wrapper that builds a token from claims and method and
returns its signed compact serialization.

funcs.name: SignDetached
funcs.signature: func SignDetached(payload []byte, method SigningMethod, key any, extraHeaders map[string]any) (string, error)
funcs.doc:
SignDetached produces a detached JWS with an unencoded payload, per RFC 7797
(the "b64":false option). The payload is signed as-is (not base64url-encoded)
and is omitted from the returned compact serialization, which has the form

	BASE64URL(header) + ".." + BASE64URL(signature)

The synthesized header sets "alg", "b64":false, and "crit":["b64"]; any
entries in extraHeaders (e.g. "kid") are merged in first. Recipients must
supply the same payload out of band and verify with VerifyDetached.

funcs.name: UnregisterSigningMethod
funcs.doc:
UnregisterSigningMethod removes the signing method registered for alg, if any.
After it returns, GetSigningMethod(alg) yields nil and the parser rejects
tokens whose header names alg. It is the inverse of RegisterSigningMethod and
is safe for concurrent use; unregistering an alg that is not present is a
no-op. Use it to lock a process down to a curated set of algorithms.

examples.doc:
Example signs a token with HS256 and then parses and verifies it, printing a
claim from the verified payload.

examples.name: SignDetached
examples.doc:
ExampleSignDetached demonstrates RFC 7797 detached, unencoded-payload JWS:
the payload is signed as-is and carried out of band rather than embedded in
the token.

examples.name: JSONWebKey (Thumbprint)
examples.doc: ExampleJSONWebKey_Thumbprint computes the RFC 7638 thumbprint of a JWK.

examples.name: ParseUnverified
examples.doc:
ExampleParseUnverified decodes a token without checking its signature, to
read a header value (here the algorithm) before verification.
